whatThisIs: Look up Remittance Advice Remark Codes (RARCs) - the supplemental codes that appear alongside Claim Adjustment Reason Codes (CARCs) on a 835 electronic remittance or paper EOB. RARCs explain why a claim or line was adjusted: N130 references medical record documentation, M86 flags a service period overlap, MA01 announces appeal rights. The catalog is maintained by the CMS-coordinated Remittance Advice Remark Code Committee; Sophie Well ships the full active list searchable by code or description fragment.
whenToUse: Use this when an EOB shows a remark code you don't recognize, when a denial pairs a CARC (the *why* of the adjustment) with one or more RARCs (the *details*), or when drafting an appeal that needs to address the specific remark the payer cited. RARCs are non-binding informational codes, but they tell you exactly which documentation, modifier, or eligibility condition the payer flagged - which is the single most useful piece of information when planning a corrected claim or appeal.
